metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2245
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.1: "Absence of the delayed mGluR1-dependent Ca2+ transient in dl20J/dl20J PN spines and hypothetical models for how myosin-Va might function to localize the ER Ca2+ store to spines"
Fig.2: "The translocation of ER into spines is disrupted in dl20J/dl20J PNs"
Fig.3: "mGFP-myosin-Va expressed in dl20J/dl20J PNs rescues ER targeting and accumulates at the tip of the spine ER"
Fig.4: "Myosin-Va is present at the leading tip of the ER tubule as the organelle translocates into a spine"

Final implementation:
'establishment of endoplasmic reticulum localization to postsynapse (GO:0099089)'
part_of 'regulation of postsynaptic cytosolic calcium levels (GO:0099566)'. Experimental description: - "Parasagittal slices (250 μm thick) were cut from the cerebellar vermis of dv/dl20J or dl20J/dl20J mice at ...."
- Overexpressed protien is of mouse species: ". Plasmids pL7-mGFP-myosin-Va and pL7-mCherry-Myosin-Va correspond to pL7-mGFP and pL7-mCherry, respectively, which contain (in frame and downstream of mGFP or mCherry) the cDNA encoding the full-length, WT, brain-spliced isoform43 of mouse myosin-Va"

checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
